title: openPullRequest is not idempotent — resume onto an existing PR branch fails at gh pr create
description:
## Goal
Make the execute-epic PR step safe to re-run. When an epic is re-triggered after a partial run (e.g. the 50-limit truncation bug closed only some tickets, or any manual resume), execute-epic re-reaches step 4 and calls openPullRequest, which runs 'gh pr create' unconditionally. If a PR already exists for the branch, gh errors and the whole run is marked failed even though all ticket commits landed and pushed.

## Context
Observed on planar-akc: first run processed 1 of 6 tickets (truncation bug, fixed in anton-noc/ae43679) and opened PR gh-30. Resuming to finish the other 5 tickets would push fine but fail on gh pr create.

## Out of scope
The --limit truncation fix (already done in ae43679).

## Verify
Unit/integration: run execute-epic twice against the same epic+branch where a PR already exists; second run finishes with run=done and reuses the existing PR ref.
acceptance_criteria: openPullRequest reuses an existing open PR for the branch (via gh pr view --head) and returns its ref instead of calling gh pr create and erroring; execute-epic resume that re-reaches step 4 with an already-open PR completes with run=done
close_reason: openPullRequest now reuses an existing open PR for the branch instead of erroring on gh pr create; covered by src/lib/git/ops.test.ts

title: Layered execution prompt: locked base + editable seed
description: When an epic runs autonomously, compose the claude --append-system-prompt from three layers: (1) a LOCKED base system prompt (code-owned, src/prompts/system-base.md) with the core operating contract — autonomous-run context, git/beads boundaries (anton owns commits + ticket stage/close + PR), collect learnings via bd remember, scope/safety; (2) the agent-tag prompt; (3) a per-project USER-EDITABLE seed prompt (settingsJson.seedPrompt) for project guidance. Add settings persistence + UI (editable seed textarea, read-only base viewer). Slim the -p ticket prompt to ticket identity + acceptance since operating rules move to the base.
acceptance_criteria: Autonomous runs always inject the locked base prompt; users can edit a per-project seed prompt that is layered on top; base+agent+seed all reach claude; verified e2e.
close_reason: Layered execution prompt: locked base contract (src/prompts/system-base.md) + agent-tag + editable per-project seed (settingsJson.seedPrompt). Composed in system-prompt.ts, wired into execute-epic, settings API+UI (editable seed textarea + read-only base viewer). Verified e2e: composition unit tests, integration proof that base+seed+agent reach claude, settings round-trip, page render.

title: node-pty session manager + SSE stream to xterm
description:
## Goal
Spawn/attach/kill an interactive claude pty; stream it to the browser xterm over SSE; track session lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: A live claude pty streams bidirectionally to the browser terminal and is cleanly torn down.

title: execute-epic job (per-ticket claude → tests → commit → PR)
description:
## Goal
For an approved epic: warm worktree, per ticket run claude → tests → commit; when tickets done open one PR (gh) and move the epic to in-review.
acceptance_criteria: Approved epic runs autonomously ticket-by-ticket and opens a single PR; stage moves to in-review.
notes: Implemented src/lib/jobs/execute-epic.ts + git/ops.ts + service wiring + instrumentation + approve->enqueue. e2e: approved epic -> worktree -> per-ticket claude/tests/commit -> single PR (gh) -> in-review.
close_reason: Closed

title: Headless claude driver (claude -p · agent prompt · stream parse)
description:
## Goal
Spawn claude -p with --append-system-prompt from the ticket agent tag, --model from settings, cwd=worktree; stream output to session log + UI (SSE); parse usage-limit signals for backoff.
acceptance_criteria: Claude runs headless per ticket with the right agent prompt/model; output streams to the UI; usage limits trigger backoff.
notes: Implemented src/lib/claude/{driver,agent-prompt}.ts. claude -p stream-json parse, agent-tag prompt injection, UsageLimitError backoff. 8 tests. Binary injectable via ANTON_CLAUDE_BIN.
close_reason: Closed

title: Git worktree manager (create · warm · cleanup)
description:
## Goal
Create/warm a git worktree + branch per epic run; clean up on completion/abort.
acceptance_criteria: Each run executes in an isolated worktree/branch; worktrees are removed after the run ends.
close_reason: Closed

title: Durable job runner (lease · backoff · poison-pill)
description:
## Goal
In-process job runner loop over the jobs table: lease queued/due jobs, run, reschedule; reclaim on lease expiry; API-limit backoff parks past reset; poison-pill parks after maxAttempts.
acceptance_criteria: Jobs are leased/reclaimed on crash; exhausted-quota jobs park + reschedule; failed jobs park for a human after N attempts.
notes: Implemented src/lib/jobs/{queue,runner,errors}.ts. Lease/reclaim, quota park+reschedule (attempt refunded), poison-pill park after maxAttempts. 13 unit tests (runner.test.ts).
close_reason: Closed

title: Board redesign: columns + epic cards (progress bar, blocked signal, agent/risk/size chips, stage left-border, PR/merged badges) + states
description: Board columns with stage dot + count pill; epic cards with id row + blocked/PR signal, title, goal, progress bar (done/total + %), agent(dot)/risk/size mono chips, stage-colored inset left border, Approve button in backlog, merged/PR badges. Redesign empty/loading(shimmer)/error states.

Topbar: breadcrumb + search (⌘K) + Filter + Add work primary button.

Add Space Grotesk display font in layout.tsx. Add keyframes: antonPulse, antonShimmer, antonBlink.

Source: claude.ai/design project 'Library experience redesign', file 'Anton Redesign.dc.html'.

title: Ticket/epic management: unified edit modal + delete + force-run
description:
## Goal

Make tickets and epics fully manageable from the UI: a single always-editable ticket modal (view + edit + save in one), delete tickets from an epic, delete an epic entirely (from detail view and from the board backlog column), and force-run an epic that is stuck in the implementing phase.

title: Epic view: wire up Run epic + Open worktree buttons

Open worktree copies the run worktree path (EpicDetail.run added via findOpenRunForEpic); disabled when no active worktree.

description: Make it easy to copy epic/ticket ids and worktree paths anywhere they appear (click-to-copy).
close_reason: Added reusable CopyButton (src/components/ui/copy-button.tsx); applied to epic id (board card + epic detail), ticket id (tickets table + ticket dialog), and epic/ticket/branch/worktree in run detail.

title: Tickets view: fixed header/filters + scrollable table body
description: Apply the same bounded-scroll pattern as the board: header, filters and footer stay pinned; the ticket rows scroll internally.
close_reason: TicketsView: header/filters/footer pinned (shrink-0), ticket rows scroll in an overflow-y-auto ul within the bounded main. Same pattern applied to epic detail.

title: Board: fixed topbar + per-lane scroll
description: On the epic board the whole view stretches the viewport instead of staying within it. Bound the app shell to viewport height so the topbar stays visible and each board lane scrolls independently.
close_reason: Bounded app shell to viewport height (h-dvh) with main as the scroll region; board lanes now scroll independently while the topbar stays fixed. Falls back to natural scroll on stacked mobile layout.

title: Edit the ticket contract (Goal / Acceptance / description) with a clean beads round-trip
description:
## Goal
Round-trip the markdown contract (Goal/Acceptance/description) through beads cleanly — the fiddly part, isolated so the low-risk field editing can ship independently.

## Why isolated
Acceptance can live in two places (the ## Acceptance section of the description OR the bead acceptance field) and parse* reads the section first. Editing naively can desync them. This ticket owns choosing and enforcing one canonical storage rule.

## Out of scope
- Reparenting, deps, labels, status (other tickets)

## Verify
Tests: edit Goal → parseGoal returns the new text; edit Acceptance → parseAcceptance returns the new items and the board/epic-detail acceptance checklist matches; a bead whose acceptance was only in the acceptance field is editable and, after save, reads consistently from one place.

## Context
touches: src/components/ticket/ticket-dialog.tsx (edit mode), and possibly beads.update/getTicketDetail passthrough from the server-layer ticket. THE SUBTLETY: parseAcceptance (src/lib/tickets.ts, src/lib/board.ts) reads the description's '## Acceptance' section FIRST, then falls back to the bead's acceptance_criteria/acceptance field. bd update has both --description and --acceptance. Pick one canonical home so an edit can't leave the two out of sync. Follow the textarea pattern in src/components/settings/settings-view.tsx.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Edit mode gains contract editing: Goal, Acceptance, and the rest of the description are editable
- [ ] Saving writes the contract to beads such that getTicketDetail / board / tickets parseGoal + parseAcceptance re-read the SAME values (no drift between the description's ## Acceptance section and the bead acceptance field)
- [ ] A single, documented storage rule is chosen and applied (e.g. the whole contract lives in --description markdown; acceptance is read from the ## Acceptance section) and existing beads that store acceptance only in the acceptance field still display and remain editable
- [ ] Long contracts scroll within the dialog; textarea styling matches the Settings seed-prompt fields
close_reason: Closed

title: Wire TicketDialog into tickets table, epic-detail list, and graph nodes
description:
## Goal
Make the popup reachable from every place a ticket appears, and keep the surfaces fresh after an edit.

## Out of scope
- The dialog internals (separate ticket)
- Opening the popup for epic rows/nodes (epics keep their detail page)

## Verify
Manual + tests: click a ticket in each of the three surfaces → same popup opens; save a change → the originating surface reflects it without a full reload. Epic rows in the tickets table still navigate to the epic page.

## Context
touches: src/components/tickets/tickets-view.tsx, src/components/epic/epic-detail-view.tsx, src/components/epic/dependency-graph.tsx. Each host holds [openTicketId,setOpenTicketId] and renders one shared TicketDialog. Reuse the existing refetch pattern (the 'attempt' counter effect in tickets-view/epic-detail-view). Preserve the current epic-row link behavior in the tickets table (only non-epic rows open the popup).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tickets table row (src/components/tickets/tickets-view.tsx): clicking a ticket opens the popup instead of navigating to the epic page; epic rows keep their current link to the epic page
- [ ] Epic-detail ticket list (src/components/epic/epic-detail-view.tsx): the 'Tickets · N' list items become clickable and open the popup
- [ ] Dependency graph (src/components/epic/dependency-graph.tsx): clicking a ticket node opens the popup (the epic node keeps its current behavior)
- [ ] On save (onSaved) the host surface refetches so the edited value shows immediately (row, list item, graph, and progress update)
- [ ] Keyboard/focus accessible: rows/items/nodes are real buttons/links with focus-visible rings consistent with existing styles
close_reason: Closed

title: TicketDialog component — view + edit (title/status/priority/agent/risk/size)
description:
## Goal
The reusable popup itself — view a ticket's contract, then edit the simple scalar/label fields and Save. Contract-markdown editing is layered on later by a separate ticket.

## Out of scope
- Editing Goal/Acceptance/description markdown (separate contract-editing ticket)
- Wiring it into the call sites (separate ticket) — this ticket may include a temporary Storybook-less harness or be exercised via the tickets-table wiring ticket

## Verify
Component test: opening with a ticketId fetches and renders view mode; entering edit mode, changing risk, and Save issues a PATCH with only { risk } and fires onSaved. Manual: dialog matches the design system in light/dark.

## Context
touches: new src/components/ticket/ticket-dialog.tsx. Reuse Dialog (src/components/ui/dialog.tsx), atoms (MetaChip/RiskChip/StagePill/Toggle), StatusCircle + SectionLabel patterns from src/components/epic/epic-detail-view.tsx, agentDotClass from board-utils, and the Settings edit+Save + sonner pattern (src/components/settings/settings-view.tsx). Match the select styling used by ModelField in settings-view.tsx. Save model is edit-then-Save (batched).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New TicketDialog (controlled: open ticketId, onClose, onSaved) built on src/components/ui/dialog.tsx
- [ ] On open it fetches GET ticket detail; shows a skeleton, then the contract in VIEW mode; has an error state
- [ ] View mode reuses the design system: id/type header, StagePill, title, MetaChip/RiskChip (agent/risk/size), PR chip, Goal, Acceptance checklist — matching epic-detail-view typography
- [ ] An Edit affordance flips to EDIT mode: title (input), status + priority (selects), agent/risk/size (selects) — Cancel and Save in the footer
- [ ] Save sends ONE PATCH of only changed fields, shows a sonner toast, calls onSaved, returns to view mode; Save disabled while pending and when nothing changed
close_reason: Closed

title: GET/PATCH ticket API route
description:
## Goal
The HTTP surface the dialog fetches and saves through: one route, GET to load, PATCH to save.

## Out of scope
- Business logic (lives in ticket-detail.ts)
- UI

## Verify
Integration/route test: GET returns a known bead's detail; PATCH with { risk: 'high' } returns detail with risk high and leaves 'approved' intact; PATCH with an unknown field 400s.

## Context
touches: new src/app/api/projects/[slug]/tickets/[ticketId]/route.ts. Mirror src/app/api/projects/[slug]/epics/[epicId]/route.ts exactly (async params, getProjectBySlug, NextResponse, force-dynamic). Depends on getTicketDetail/updateTicket from the server-layer ticket.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] GET /api/projects/[slug]/tickets/[ticketId] returns { detail: TicketDetail } (404 on unknown project/ticket)
- [ ] PATCH same route accepts a validated field patch and calls updateTicket, returning the refreshed { detail }
- [ ] PATCH validates status/priority/label values server-side and rejects unknown fields
- [ ] Route is dynamic (force-dynamic) and reads the project via getProjectBySlug, matching the epics route
close_reason: Closed

title: Beads write path + ticket-detail server layer
description:
## Goal
The server foundation everything else builds on: a typed ticket-detail read and a single-command beads write that only touches the fields you actually changed.

## Key risk — label preservation
A bead carries mixed labels: managed metadata (agent:/risk:/size:/domain:) AND control labels (approved, stage:implementing/in-review, source:*). Editing 'risk' must remove-label the old risk:* and add-label the new one WITHOUT touching approved/stage/source. Never use --set-labels blindly. Compute the diff from the bead's current labels.

## Out of scope
- The API route (separate ticket) and any UI
- Editing the description markdown sections (Goal/Acceptance round-trip) — that's the contract-editing ticket; this layer just exposes --description/--acceptance passthrough

## Verify
Unit tests: beads.update builds the expected bd argv for each patch shape (title only; status+priority; agent change from nextjs→fastapi keeps 'approved'); a no-field patch produces no write. getTicketDetail returns parsed goal/acceptance/labels for a task bead.

## Context
touches: src/lib/beads/bd.ts, src/lib/types.ts, new src/lib/ticket-detail.ts. Follow the read/parse patterns in src/lib/epic-detail.ts and src/lib/tickets.ts (parseGoal/parseAcceptance/labelValue). bd write helpers already exist (tag/untag/setStatus); this adds a single generic patch. Label prefixes: agent, risk, size, domain. Priority format is 0-4 / P0-P4. deriveStage (src/lib/board.ts) couples status→stage — changing status here is a legit manual override.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] TicketDetail type added to src/lib/types.ts (Ticket + type, priority, domain, goal, description, epicId, epicTitle)
- [ ] beads.update(cwd, id, patch) added to bd.ts: maps a field patch to ONE bd update invocation (--title, --status, --priority, --acceptance, --description, and add/remove-label)
- [ ] Label edits diff ONLY the agent:/risk:/size:/domain: prefix being changed and preserve all other labels (approved, stage:*, source:*)
- [ ] getTicketDetail(project, id) and updateTicket(project, id, patch) added (new src/lib/ticket-detail.ts), mirroring epic-detail.ts read patterns
- [ ] Empty/undefined patch fields are no-ops (never clobber an unedited field)
close_reason: Closed

title: Ticket detail popup: inspect & edit a ticket, writing back to beads
description:
## Goal
Give anton a single ticket-detail popup that lets you inspect a ticket's full contract and edit its key fields in place, writing changes straight to beads — so you can triage (fix agent/risk/size, flip status, tweak the Goal/Acceptance) without leaving the board or the tickets table.

## Why
Today no surface opens an individual ticket: the tickets table title links to the parent *epic* page, the epic-detail ticket list is non-interactive, and every field is read-only. The only way to correct a mis-shaped ticket is the bd CLI. This closes that loop inside anton's UI while keeping beads authoritative.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. A shared TicketDialog (view + edit), a thin beads write path (beads.update → one bd update), a GET/PATCH ticket API, and wiring at three call sites. Edit-then-Save batching (chosen), not per-field autosave.

## Out of scope
- Creating or deleting tickets from the popup (shaping stays in /shape; approval/PR stay where they are)
- Editing epics' own fields via this popup (epics keep their detail page; the popup targets tickets, though it may open for orphan single-ticket cards)
- Reparenting / editing dependency edges
- Realtime/multi-client sync

## Verify
Each child ticket carries its own Verify. End to end: open the popup from all three entry points, edit each field type, confirm the value round-trips through bd (bd show) and re-renders on the board + tickets table + epic detail.

## Context
beads is the single source of truth (DESIGN.md §3): anton reads/writes via bd, never duplicates state. Touches src/lib/beads/bd.ts, src/lib/types.ts, a new src/lib/ticket-detail.ts, a new API route, a new TicketDialog component, and the three call sites (tickets-view, epic-detail-view, dependency-graph).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] One TicketDialog opens from the tickets table, the epic-detail ticket list, and the dependency-graph nodes
- [ ] View mode shows the full ticket contract (id/type/stage, title, agent/risk/size/PR, Goal, Acceptance, linked epic)
- [ ] Edit mode lets you change title, status, priority, agent/risk/size, and the Goal/Acceptance/description contract; Save writes one bd update
- [ ] Every edit is reflected in beads (bd) and re-renders correctly across the board, tickets table, and epic detail
- [ ] Follows the design system (Dialog + atoms + epic-detail typography)
close_reason: Closed

title: Board/epic views truncated: bd list default limit 50 drops tickets in large repos
description:
## Problem
anton's beads.list()/ready() don't pass --limit, so bd caps results at its default 50. In a repo with >50 issues (planar has 211), the board + epic detail + autonomous jobs (execute-epic, review-fix, orphan-grooming) see only the first 50 beads. Epics show partial children (planar-8lp: 3 of 5; security-hardening planar-akc: 1 of 6) and wrong completion (looks 'done' because only closed children survived the window).

## Root cause
src/lib/beads/bd.ts list/ready run 'bd list --json' / 'bd ready --json' with no '--limit 0'.

## Secondary bug
Orphan (parentless) non-epic beads are wrapped as pseudo-epic cards on the board (ticketAsEpic) but getEpicDetail throws for non-epics -> clicking them 404s (observed: /epics/planar-yhx, a bug).

## Fix
- beads.list/ready pass --limit 0 (unlimited).
- getEpicDetail renders a non-epic bead as a single-ticket detail instead of 404.
acceptance_criteria: All of an epic's tickets show regardless of repo size; completion % correct; orphan cards open instead of 404
close_reason: beads.list/ready pass --limit 0 (bd defaults to 50; planar has 211 → epics were truncated). getEpicDetail renders orphan non-epic beads as single-ticket detail instead of 404. Verified vs real planar data: 8lp 3→5, security 1→6, planar-yhx no longer 404s. 3 regression tests added; 171 tests pass.

title: Pre-commit hook (husky + lint-staged: eslint + typecheck)
acceptance_criteria: committing runs eslint --fix on staged ts/tsx + tsc --noEmit; blocks on failure
close_reason: husky + lint-staged pre-commit: eslint --fix staged ts/tsx + tsc --noEmit; prepare script installs on bun install; verified it blocks a type error (exit 2).

title: Rewrite README (what/how-to-use/how-to-run)
acceptance_criteria: README describes anton, the core loop, prereqs, anton setup/start, env vars
close_reason: README rewritten: what anton is, prereqs, install/run (anton setup/start), the core loop, config/env vars, development (CI/pre-commit/tests).

title: CI workflow (lint+typecheck+test+build on push/PR)
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on push+PR: install, lint, typecheck, test, build
close_reason: .github/workflows/ci.yml: lint+typecheck+test+build on push(main)+PR, with concurrency cancel. Gates verified green locally.

title: Dev tooling: CI, pre-commit, and real README
description:
## Goal
Round out the project's engineering hygiene: a CI workflow (lint+typecheck+test+build on push/PR) complementing the tag release CD, a pre-commit hook (lint + typecheck on staged files), and a real README replacing the create-next-app boilerplate.

## Acceptance
- [ ] CI runs lint, typecheck, test, build on push + PR and passes
- [ ] pre-commit runs lint + typecheck locally before each commit
- [ ] README explains what anton is, how to use it, how to run it
acceptance_criteria: CI green + pre-commit configured + README rewritten
close_reason: CI + pre-commit + README all shipped on feat/anton-3t2-phase2-jobs; lint+typecheck+test(168)+build all green.

title: TESTPROBE delete me
close_reason: probe

title: Editable review-fix reasoning prompt (default file + settings override)
acceptance_criteria: src/prompts/review-fix.md default; ProjectSettings.reviewFixPrompt override; job loads reasoning + injects PR context; settings API+UI expose it
close_reason: src/prompts/review-fix.md default + ProjectSettings.reviewFixPrompt override; job loads reasoning + appends PR context; settings API+UI expose it; e2e tested (default + override).

acceptance_criteria: anton setup runs prereq checks + drizzle migrate + node-pty rebuild; anton start builds+starts the server; wired via package.json bin

title: anton ops: CLI (setup/start) + editable review-fix prompt + release CI
description:
## Goal
Make anton turn-key like foolery: an `anton` CLI (setup/start/doctor/dev) shipped via npm bin, an editable review-fix reasoning prompt (default file, overridable per-project in settings like seedPrompt), and a tag-triggered release CI that runs gates + cuts a GitHub Release.

## Acceptance
- [ ] anton setup/start/doctor/dev work via npm bin launcher
- [ ] review-fix job loads an editable reasoning prompt (settings override + default file)
- [ ] release workflow on tag v* runs lint+test+build and creates a GitHub Release
acceptance_criteria: CLI + editable review-fix prompt + release CI all shipped and tested
close_reason: All 3 children shipped on feat/anton-3t2-phase2-jobs: anton CLI (npm bin), editable review-fix prompt (default file + settings override + API/UI), release CI on tag. Full build + 168 tests + tsc + lint green.

title: Run detail: live terminal + meta grid + timeline
description:
## Goal
Wire the /runs detail screen: live terminal, run meta grid (worktree/branch/model/agent/lease), and event timeline.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a run shows its live terminal, metadata, and a timeline of events.
close_reason: Run detail page: live xterm terminal (SSE), run meta grid, session timeline; attach logic extracted + unit-tested; verified via prod build + real HTTP/SSE

title: Runs API + SSE run-log stream
description:
## Goal
List/detail runs from anton.db and stream a run's live session log over SSE to the client.
acceptance_criteria: The Runs screen loads real runs and streams live log output for an active run.
close_reason: Runs list/detail API + SSE session-log stream (replay+tail) with tail lib and 6 tests

title: review-fix job (poll PR via gh · dispatch claude to resolve)
description:
## Goal
For in-review runs: poll the PR for review comments + CI via gh; when actionable, dispatch claude in the worktree to resolve, push, re-request review.
acceptance_criteria: Actionable PR review comments/CI failures are auto-resolved by claude and pushed.
close_reason: review-fix job: poll in-review PRs via gh, classify actionable (changes-requested/failing CI), re-materialize worktree, dispatch claude to fix, commit, push, comment + re-request review. e2e tested (3 cases inc. unpushed-prior-commit).

title: nightly-stringer (scan → scan-triage → beads)
description:
## Goal
On cron: stringer scan --delta → /scan-triage prompt → create beads per project.
acceptance_criteria: Nightly scan findings are triaged into beads automatically per project.
close_reason: nightly-stringer job: stringer scan --delta → /scan-triage claude prompt → beads per project. e2e tested (triage-to-beads + zero-signal no-op).

title: Schedules table + cron scheduler loop
description:
## Goal
Cron-driven scheduler that enqueues due jobs (nightly stringer, review-fix polling) from the schedules table.
acceptance_criteria: Enabled schedules enqueue their jobs at the right cron times; lastRun/nextRun tracked.
close_reason: cron parser + scheduler loop + schedules service implemented; 15 tests pass

title: Wire /shape UI to live pty; draft epic → backlog bead on accept
description:
## Goal
Replace the static ShapeView terminal with the live pty; on accept, create an open (unapproved) bead in backlog from the draft.
acceptance_criteria: Shaping is a real conversation in the browser; accepting creates an open bead in backlog.

title: Run & session persistence (runs/sessions rows · leases)
description:
## Goal
Persist a run (worktree/branch/model/agent/attempts/lease) and its claude sessions to anton.db for the Runs UI and recovery.
acceptance_criteria: Every run + session is recorded with enough state to resume and to render the Runs screen.
notes: Extended src/lib/runs.ts (create/update/findOpenRunForEpic) + new src/lib/sessions.ts (+ log files). Runs+sessions recorded; resume proven in e2e.
close_reason: Closed

title: Persist project settings (agents, concurrency, automation, remove-project)
description: Settings UI (src/components/settings/settings-view.tsx) is presentational. Persist to projects.settings_json: active agents, max-concurrency, autonomous flag, automation toggles; wire a DELETE for remove-project. Save button currently only toasts.

title: Settings page (NEW route): subnav + General/Agents/Execution/Automation/Danger panels
description: New /projects/[slug]/settings route: left subnav (General/Agents/Execution/Automation/Danger zone) + panels: general fields, active-agents toggles grid, execution (max concurrent slider + autonomy toggle), automation toggles, danger-zone remove project.
close_reason: settings subnav + general/agents/execution/automation/danger panels

title: Runs page (NEW route): runs list + live terminal + run meta + timeline
description: New /projects/[slug]/runs (+ /runs/[runId]) route: sidebar job-queue section, executing status header (Take over/Stop), left live terminal stream, right run meta grid (worktree/branch/model/agent/attempts/lease) + vertical timeline.
close_reason: runs list from DB + status rows + honest empty state

title: Add work / Shape page (NEW route): interactive terminal pane + live draft-epic panel
description: New /projects/[slug]/shape route: split layout — left terminal (shaping conversation transcript, connected status) + right draft-epic panel (TITLE/GOAL/PROPOSED TICKETS/chips) with 'Send to backlog' CTA. Wire Add work buttons to it.
close_reason: shape page: terminal pane + draft panel + send-to-backlog

title: Projects redesign: workspace home cards (icon tile, path, epics/active-runs stats) + add-project dialog restyle
description: Project rows with mono icon tile, name, repo path · branch, epics count + active runs stat. Restyle add-project dialog: repository path field, name + default branch, green '.beads/ found · N issues' confirmation banner.
close_reason: projects home rows + dialog restyle

title: Epic detail redesign: header actions + contract panel (completion module, goal, acceptance checklist, tickets) + themed graph
description: Two-column layout: left contract panel (stage pill header + Pause run/Open worktree actions, big completion module with done/in-progress/blocked segmented bar + legend, GOAL, ACCEPTANCE checklist, TICKETS list with status circles), right dependency graph with themed nodes (left-border = ticket stage) on dotted canvas.
close_reason: epic detail two-col contract+completion module+acceptance checklist+themed graph

title: Tickets redesign: filter toolbar (search + active filter pills + clear) + dense table + footer
description: Filter toolbar: search box, active filter pills (removable), add-filter dashed button, Clear all. Dense grid table: status circle (done check / in-progress dot / blocked lock / todo ring), title (strikethrough when done), mono id, epic, agent dot, risk, size; zebra rows; footer count + prev/next.
close_reason: tickets dense table + status circles + full-bleed toolbar + footer

title: Ticket dependency graph (React Flow)
description:
## Goal
Graph of tickets within the epic using @xyflow/react + dagre layout.

## Acceptance
- [ ] nodes=tickets, edges=deps, auto-laid-out, theme-aware
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Epic detail page UI
description:
## Goal
Detail page: full contract + tickets list with status/metadata.

## Acceptance
- [ ] click epic on board -> detail page with all tickets
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Epic detail API (epic + tickets + dep edges)
description:
## Goal
GET epic detail: the epic, its tickets, and their dependency edges.

## Acceptance
- [ ] returns epic + tickets + edges; tested
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Epic detail + ticket dependency graph
description:
## Goal
Click an epic to open a detail view: full contract (goal/acceptance/description), all related tickets with their status/metadata, and a dependency graph of the tickets within that epic (parent-child / blocks / related edges).
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] clicking an epic opens a detail view with all its tickets + a dependency graph
close_reason: All child tickets complete and verified live (board, epic detail+graph, tickets+filters, drag-and-drop).

title: Phase 2 jobs: review-fix, nightly stringer, orphan grooming
description:
## Goal
Background jobs: review-fix (PR comments/CI), nightly stringer -> scan-triage, orphan-ticket grooming.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] review-fix + nightly stringer feed + orphan grooming jobs
close_reason: All 4 child tickets implemented + e2e-tested on branch feat/anton-3t2-phase2-jobs: cron scheduler loop, review-fix, nightly-stringer, orphan-grooming. Handlers registered + scheduler started in service; default schedules seeded per project. Specialist-reviewed; 6 findings addressed. 164 tests pass, tsc + lint clean.

title: Runs, diagnostics, history
description:
## Goal
Runs list/detail, live xterm attach to sessions, and diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] see active runs + history + live xterm attach + diagnostics
close_reason: All children complete: Runs API+SSE stream, run detail (live xterm terminal + meta grid + timeline), history & diagnostics. Active runs + history + live attach + diagnostics all delivered and verified e2e.

title: Interactive shaping (/shape + xterm)
description:
## Goal
An interactive claude pty session (xterm) seeded with src/prompts/shape.md produces epics+tickets in beads.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Add work opens an interactive claude session that lands epics in backlog

title: Autonomous execution (execute-epic job + runner)
description:
## Goal
Durable job runner: approved epic -> warm worktree -> claude -p (agent tag prompt) -> tests -> PR -> in-review, with API-limit backoff + poison-pill parking.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] approved epics run autonomously -> worktree -> PR -> in-review
close_reason: All 5 child tickets implemented + e2e-tested on branch feat/anton-dzh-autonomous-execution. Full pipeline proven: approved epic -> worktree -> PR -> in-review.

title: Tickets page UI + filter controls
description:
## Goal
Table/list of tickets with filter controls; links to the epic; theme-aware.

## Acceptance
- [ ] filterable table, URL-synced filters, links to epics
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Tickets page + filters
description:
## Goal
A tickets view: every ticket across the project, filterable by beads metadata (agent, risk, size, domain, status, type, epic, text).
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] a page lists individual tickets, filterable by beads metadata
close_reason: All child tickets complete and verified live (board, epic detail+graph, tickets+filters, drag-and-drop).

title: Tickets query + filter API
description:
## Goal
GET tickets with filter params; returns flat ticket rows with metadata + epic ref.

## Acceptance
- [ ] filters by agent/risk/size/domain/status/type/epic/text; tested
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Stage-change API (move -> bead ops)
description:
## Goal
POST move: target stage -> bead ops (backlog=clear labels/reopen; implementing=stage:implementing; in-review=stage:in-review; done=close).

## Acceptance
- [ ] endpoint maps each stage to the right bd mutation; tested
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: dnd-kit: drag cards between stage columns
description:
## Goal
Integrate dnd-kit; drag cards across columns; optimistic move + revert on error.

## Acceptance
- [ ] smooth drag between columns; keyboard-accessible; optimistic
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Board drag-and-drop
description:
## Goal
Move epic/orphan cards between the four stage columns to change their stage, persisted to beads.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] cards drag between stage columns and persist via beads
close_reason: All child tickets complete and verified live (board, epic detail+graph, tickets+filters, drag-and-drop).

title: Polish pass: empty/loading states, motion, focus
description:
## Goal
Skeletons, empty states, hover/focus, subtle motion.

## Acceptance
- [ ] loading skeletons + empty states + accessible focus
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Redesign board columns + epic cards
description:
## Goal
Visual polish: hierarchy, density, badges, states — theme-aware.

## Acceptance
- [ ] board + cards look polished in both themes
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: App shell: nav + layout + design tokens
description:
## Goal
Sidebar/topbar nav (Projects, Board, Tickets), consistent container, refined type scale + spacing + color tokens.

## Acceptance
- [ ] shared shell wraps all pages; nav works; tokens theme-aware
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: next-themes + dark/light/system toggle
description:
## Goal
Add next-themes ThemeProvider + a toggle in the shell; respect system; no flash.

## Acceptance
- [ ] theme persists, toggles light/dark/system, no FOUC
close_reason: Implemented & verified: shipped in the app (routes return 200, tests/lint/build green) and refined in the Atelier redesign.

title: Design overhaul + dark mode
description:
## Goal
Make anton look genuinely good: a cohesive design system, an app shell with nav, and full dark mode. This is the primary UX surface.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] app looks polished and cohesive in light AND dark mode
close_reason: All child tickets complete and verified live (board, epic detail+graph, tickets+filters, drag-and-drop).

title: Run history & diagnostics (past sessions · logs)
description:
## Goal
Browse completed runs and their session logs for diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Past runs and their logs are viewable for debugging.
close_reason: History/diagnostics: runs list grouped Active/History with timestamps+duration; run-detail timeline replays any past session log via SSE; failed runs surface error. Verified e2e.

title: orphan-grooming job (bucket loose tickets)
description:
## Goal
Tickets with no epic get bucketed into an epic (or fixed in a single PR).
acceptance_criteria: Orphan tickets are periodically grouped under an epic.
close_reason: orphan-grooming job: bucket open non-epic tickets with no parent under a reusable grooming epic. e2e tested (bucketing + idempotency).

title: Live run terminal + run detail (meta grid + timeline) on Runs
description: Runs index (src/app/projects/[slug]/runs) lists runs from anton.db. Add a run-detail route streaming the headless claude session log over SSE into a terminal, plus the run meta grid (worktree/branch/model/agent/lease) and event timeline from the design's screen 09.

title: Wire /shape to a real interactive pty + SSE (draft epic → backlog)
description: The Add-work/Shape screen (src/components/shape/shape-view.tsx) has the full UI. Wire it to a node-pty claude session streamed over SSE to an xterm, forming the draft epic live and creating the open bead on 'Send to backlog'. See DESIGN.md §5.

title: Cover / design-system reference page + verify build, tests, lint
description: Optional /design internal reference page mirroring the doc's foundations/components/states. Run quality gates: bun test, bun lint, bun build; fix regressions.
close_reason: n/a — folding verification into this pass
